A2 Key for Schools — Practice Test 2
Units 5–8: What We Wear · Mix and Mash · Cool Apps and Gadgets · Into the Past
 
 
 
PAPER 1: READING AND WRITING
Time: 1 hour 10 minutes
READING
Part 1   Questions 1–6
For each question, choose the correct answer.
1
School Uniform Shop
All uniforms half price this weekend only. Open Saturday 9 a.m. – 5 p.m.
The uniform shop has a sale every weekend.
You can buy cheaper uniforms this Saturday.
The shop is only open in the morning.
2
From: Lily   To: Oscar
Have you downloaded that new music app? You can mix different songs together. I tried it last night — it's really fun!
Lily wants Oscar to make a song for her.
Lily is telling Oscar about an app she enjoyed using.
Lily is asking Oscar to help her fix a music app.
3
History Museum
Free entry for school groups on Tuesdays. Please book at least one week before your visit.
School groups can visit for free any day.
Schools must pay to visit on Tuesdays.
Schools need to book ahead to get free entry on Tuesdays.
4
Gadget Fair — This Saturday
Come and try the latest tablets, smartphones and robots! Town Hall, 10 a.m. – 4 p.m.
You can buy and try new technology this Saturday.
The fair only sells robots.
The fair is at the school on Saturday.
5
From: Dad   To: Mia
I've found some old photos of Grandma when she was young. She's wearing a really funny hat! Do you want to see them?
Dad wants Mia to buy a hat for Grandma.
Dad is showing Mia pictures of himself as a child.
Dad has found old pictures and wants to share them with Mia.
6
NOTICE
This phone charging station is free to use. Please do not leave your device unattended.
You need to pay to charge your phone here.
You can charge your phone for free, but you should stay with it.
You cannot use this charging station today.
BAYETAV Schools · A2 Key for Schools Practice Test 2 · Paper 1: Reading & Writing · Page 1
Part 2   Questions 7–13
Read the text about three teenagers and how they use technology. For each question, choose the correct answer.
A — Yuki
Yuki loves making videos. She uses her tablet to record short films about fashion and shares them on the internet. She has been interested in clothes and accessories since she was very young. "My grandmother taught me how to make jewellery when I was eight," she says. "Now I combine old and new styles in my videos. I think mixing traditional and modern fashion is really cool."
B — Matteo
Matteo is crazy about music apps. He downloads different sounds and mixes them together on his smartphone to create new songs. He prefers electronic music but also likes traditional instruments. "I love combining old instruments like the guitar with modern electronic beats," he says. "My dream is to produce music for films in the future."
C — Suki
Suki uses her laptop to learn about history. She reads online articles about ancient civilisations and watches documentaries about how people lived hundreds of years ago. "I think the past is fascinating," she says. "I've learned that people in the past were very clever. They invented things we still use today. I want to become an archaeologist one day."
Write the correct letter (A, B or C) in the box.
7
Who wants to work in the film industry?
8
Who is interested in things that happened a long time ago?
9
Who learned a skill from a family member?
10
Who likes to put together different types of music?
11
Who shares creative work online?
12
Who wants to study old objects and places in the future?
13
Who uses a phone to make music?
Part 3   Questions 14–18
Read the text and answer the questions. For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).
The History of School Uniforms
School uniforms have been part of education for hundreds of years. The first school uniforms appeared in England in the 16th century. Students at Christ's Hospital School in London wore long blue coats, and the school still uses a similar uniform today.

For many years, only rich children wore school uniforms. In the 19th century, uniforms became more common in schools across Britain. By the 20th century, most schools in the UK had some kind of dress code. Other countries, including Japan, Australia and many African nations, also introduced uniforms.

Some people believe uniforms are a good idea because all students look the same, so nobody feels different because of their clothes. Uniforms can also be cheaper than buying fashionable clothes for school every year. However, other people think students should be free to choose what they wear. They say clothes are a way to express your personality.

Today, the debate about school uniforms continues. Some schools are making their uniforms more modern and comfortable, using better fabrics and allowing students to choose between trousers and skirts.
14 When did school uniforms first appear?
A   In the 15th century.
B   In the 16th century.
C   In the 19th century.
15 In the beginning, who wore school uniforms?
A   All children.
B   Only children from wealthy families.
C   Only children in London.
16 Why do some people think uniforms are good?
A   Because they are always fashionable.
B   Because students can express themselves.
C   Because they make students equal and can save money.
17 What do people who disagree with uniforms say?
A   Uniforms are too expensive.
B   Students should choose their own clothes to show who they are.
C   Uniforms are not comfortable.
18 What are some schools doing with their uniforms now?
A   Getting rid of them completely.
B   Making them more modern and comfortable.
C   Making them more expensive.
BAYETAV Schools · A2 Key for Schools Practice Test 2 · Paper 1: Reading & Writing · Page 2
Part 4   Questions 19–24
Read the text. For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).
Life in Ancient Egypt

Ancient Egypt was one of the (19) ___ amazing civilisations in history. People lived along the River Nile and (20) ___ farming, building and creating art. Egyptian children didn't go to school like we do today, (21) ___ some learned to read and write at home.

The Egyptians (22) ___ incredible things, including huge pyramids and beautiful jewellery. They (23) ___ believed that life continued after death, so they buried people with food, clothes and (24) ___ valuable objects.
19.   A more   B most   C much
20.   A enjoyed   B enjoy   C are enjoying
21.   A so   B because   C but
22.   A build   B built   C building
23.   A too   B also   C as well
24.   A another   B other   C others
Part 5   Questions 25–30
Read the email. For each question, write ONE word to fill the gap.
From: Lucas   To: Amara

Hi Amara,
Yesterday I went to (25)   technology fair with my family. It (26)   really exciting! We saw some incredible robots that (27)   dance and play music. I also tried (28)   virtual reality headset — it felt like I was (29)   inside a video game!

Have you (30)   been to a technology fair? You should try it!

Lucas
WRITING
Part 6   Question 31
Your English friend wants to know about your favourite gadget or piece of technology. Write an email to your friend.
Write 25 words or more. In your email:
• say what your favourite gadget is    • explain what you use it for    • say why you like it

Look at the three pictures. Write a story about finding something old and interesting.
